workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Can you extract the forecast prep from this meeting transcript for Woodgrove Labs?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-01 16:23 EST
Subject: Follow-up: forecast prep Review

Our team encountered some challenges with forecast prep. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
Date: 2024-02-07 12:57 EST
Subject: Re: Re: forecast prep Timeline

Just checking in on the forecast prep proposal. Our CFO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-01 10:41 EST
Subject: URGENT: forecast prep Issue at Woodgrove Labs

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with forecast prep. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-I trap (Exposes internal blame in customer draft); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-I failure mode (Exposes internal blame in customer draft)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-I -- Exposes internal blame in customer draft
